danger_signals.visual_form: Corrupt police officers and hostile creatures rendered in rotoscoped animation
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter imminent; player must defend or avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Multi-level altitude platforms, gaps between screens, falling distances
danger_signals.meaning: Death from fall damage if Conrad drops from great height; spatial navigation threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Visually disguised as humans in authority positions (police, officials)
danger_signals.meaning: Deception in world-state; apparent authority figures are alien threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Force shield absorption visual feedback during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Health/protection status; shield recharging needed before continued exposure

danger_signals.visual_form: Unreachable ledges, barriers, spatial dead-ends in level layout
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; puzzle or equipment requirement needed

safety_signals.visual_form: Ian character model in New Washington; friendly NPCs distinct from enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safe zone; support and information availability

safety_signals.visual_form: Pistol with unlimited ammunition carried by Conrad
Deployable barrier visual effect from equipment
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary defensive tool; can block incoming fire to enable movement

safety_signals.visual_form: Late-game equipment; throwable object with teleport effect
safety_signals.meaning: Spatial puzzle solution; access to previously unreachable areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Jungle terrain on Titan, urban New Washington city streets without immediate threats
safety_signals.meaning: Exploration and navigation safe; information gathering opportunity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rendered as authority figures but are revealed as Morph agents or compromised
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban New Washington police presence; official government structures

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: No visible sigil; identity is concealment
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Disguised as human police, officials, and authority figures; true form not visually distinct until revealed in hideout vent scene
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hideout beneath nightclub; teleportation technology; presence indicated by suspicious authority behavior
New Washington city; safe zones where allies operate; gladiatorial Death Tower arena (neutral but dangerous)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Conrad amnesiac and vulnerable on Titan
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jungle environment; isolated protagonist in alien terrain; self-directed exploration without context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Conrad moves cautiously; discovers recording device to recover memory; vulnerability to fall damage and environmental threats

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Morph infiltration of human society (unknown to authorities)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Authority figures (police) behave suspiciously; attacks on innocents (Ian); visual disconnect between uniform authority and actions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police aggression toward unarmed civilians; Morphs coordinate pursuit of Conrad; hidden coordination beneath normal society

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Conrad gathering intelligence and forged papers
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multi-screen urban environment with various NPCs and locations; Death Tower gladiatorial arena as central objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Conrad undertakes dangerous jobs; must navigate social hierarchy and criminal underworld; preparation phase before confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nightclub underground location; technological alien architecture; vent shaft overlooking Morph operations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escape sequence; forced combat; evidence of Morph technology and conquest plans observed; final confrontation approach

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Portal to Morph home planet accessible
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Teleportation device becomes visual objective; access to previously unreachable dimensional space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Conrad actively pursues confrontation; rescue of human prisoner Phillip Howard Clark; invasion of alien territory

environmental_storytelling.example: Conrad finds self-made recording in jungle advising him to find Ian; regenerator device triggers memory-based narrative exposition
Ian being attacked by police signals authority corruption; Morph infiltration visible through suspicious official actions
Jungle → New Washington city streets → criminal underworld → Morph hideout → alien planet; geography reflects discovery and escalation
Teleportation device acquired late-game unlocks spatial solutions; force field and shield depict changing defensive needs
Morph hideout beneath nightclub shows infiltration depth; alien technology visible in final locations distinguishes Morph spaces from human areas

environmental_storytelling.technique: Spatial puzzle design as narrative barrier
environmental_storytelling.example: Multi-level altitude challenges reflect difficulty of navigation through corrupted society; unreachable areas symbolize secrets and infiltration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Cautious exploration; realistic human movement speed and jumping; realistic weakness to fall damage; defensive pistol usage
Active pursuit of Morphs; acceptance of dangerous jobs; willingness to infiltrate hideout; use of teleportation device for invasion
Attack Ian without provocation; pursue Conrad; aggressive response to citizen questions; unified coordination suggests external control
Under attack at introduction; possesses memory restoration technology; provides information and support; aids in forged paper acquisition
Erase Conrad's memory; pursue and capture him; disguise as human authorities; plan technological subversion of Earth; defend home planet access
Imprisoned in Morph hideout; awaits rescue; represents stakes of Morph conquest plan

notes: Flashback communicates world-state primarily through cinematic visual storytelling and spatial design. Danger is conveyed through enemy placement, environmental hazards, and the visual disconnect between authority uniforms and suspicious behavior (revealing infiltration). The Morph faction's primary visual signal is deception—they appear as trusted authorities but act with predatory intent. Political situation (infiltration of government) emerges through NPC behavior and location narrative rather than explicit text. The game uses rotoscoped animation to create visual clarity in character state and intent. Progression through increasingly dangerous locations mirrors plot escalation from amnesia → discovery → confrontation. Equipment availability (shield, teleportation device) provides visual feedback on capability progression. The multi-level altitude puzzle design reflects the difficulty of navigating a compromised world.
